description: A Python-based framework designed to quantify risk in Bitcoin trading using Probabilistic Machine Learning. Most ML models fail in crypto because they ignore oratorical uncertainty (the inherent randomness of the market). This repo provides tools to build models that don't just predict the next move

A systematic quantitative research pipeline designed to extract alpha from stock returns. This project integrates multi-modal data (fundamental, technical, and alternative) into a unified machine learning framework to generate high-conviction trading signals

This repository provides a complete end-to-end framework for forecasting hourly intraday electricity prices on the Iberian market and converting these forecasts into systematic trading strategies. The project integrates data engineering, advanced machine-learning models, hyperparameter optimisation, and algorithmic trading mechanisms.

TERV is an advanced Machine Learning validation and comparison framework designed to build, evaluate, and explain multiple classification models in a transparent and interpretable way.  It integrates traditional ML algorithms such as:  Linear Models  Logistic Regression  K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)  Naive Bayes  Decision Tree  Random Forest

It emphasizes clean architecture, reproducible pipelines, model evaluation, optimization, and API integration to transform experimental ML workflows into production-ready systems.

ASEADOSSDNIoT, a novel and publicly available intrusion detection dataset supported by a fully documented hybrid testbed framework. The resulting dataset contains 457,044 labeled flow instances with 83 statistical features and was evaluated using state-of-the-art machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques.

End-to-end machine learning framework for diabetes prediction. Leverages Local Outlier Factor (LOF) for anomaly detection and 10-fold cross-validation for model selection. Features an optimized ensemble of LightGBM and Gradient Boosting tuned via GridSearchCV for maximum sensitivity.

High-throughput machine learning pipeline designed to decode the regulatory logic of microexon splicing. The framework uses Support Vector Regression (SVR) to predict microexon inclusion levels (PSI) based on the expression profiles of Splicing Factors (SFs), utilizing SHAP values to identify the primary regulatory drivers.

MLOptimizer is a comprehensive framework for automatically generating optimized machine learning models while capturing detailed hardware performance metrics. It provides a distributed system for model training, hyperparameter optimization, and hardware resource monitoring.

A Python-based machine learning framework that predicts Tesla's daily stock returns by combining FinBERT transformer sentiment analysis of Elon Musk's tweets with traditional OHLCV technical indicators. The sentiment-enhanced model achieved a statistically significant R improvement of 0.5% over the baseline, validated through bootstrap resampling.

CircumSpectrum is a flux-based framework that converts bulk and single-cell RNA-seq data into metabolic fingerprints using genome-scale models and machine learning. It includes RADAR, SOURIS, and CORALIE modules for reaction-level, cross-cohort, and multi-tier correlation analyses.
